module-8-account-tracker-project.label: Module 8 Account Tracker Project
module-8-account-tracker-project.summary: Create a multifile account tracker with a model file, formatting helper, data file, and thin main script.

module_8_account_tracker_step6.title: Create Main Script to Test Account

module_8_account_tracker_step1.title: Define the Account Class
module_8_account_tracker_step1.prompt: Start the module project. In step 1 of 6, Create a class `Account` in a file named `models/account.py`. The class should have an `__init__` method that initializes `name` and `balance` attributes. Ensure the class encapsulates these attributes properly.
module_8_account_tracker_step1.hint: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.
help.hint_1: This is the first project step. Build a clean starting version, then run it before moving on.
help.hint_2: Click Run and compare the output panel with the expected result.

module_8_account_tracker_step2.title: Add Deposit Method
module_8_account_tracker_step2.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 2 of 6, Enhance the `Account` class by adding a `deposit` method that increases the account balance. The method should take an amount as a parameter and update the balance accordingly.
module_8_account_tracker_step2.hint: Begin with the working code from the previous step. Keep that code, follow the new comments, and add only the next focused behavior.

help.concept: This module project is progressive. Each step starts from the previous working solution and adds one focused feature.

module_8_account_tracker_step2.starterCode:
# models/account.py

class Account:
    def __init__(self, name, balance):
        self.name = name
        self.balance = balance

module_8_account_tracker_step3.title: Add Withdraw Method with Validation
module_8_account_tracker_step3.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 3 of 6, Add a `withdraw` method to the `Account` class that decreases the balance. Ensure the method checks if the balance is sufficient before withdrawing.

module_8_account_tracker_step3.starterCode:
# models/account.py

class Account:
    def __init__(self, name, balance):
        self.name = name
        self.balance = balance

    def deposit(self, amount):
        self.balance += amount
# Project step 3: Add Withdraw Method with Validation
# Keep the working code above from the previous step.
# Next, Add a `withdraw` method to the `Account` class that decreases the balance
# Add only the focused change for this step below or inside the existing work.

module_8_account_tracker_step4.title: Create a Helper for Formatting
module_8_account_tracker_step4.prompt: Continue the same module project from the previous working step. In step 4 of 6, Create a helper function `format_currency` in a file named `helpers/formatting.py`. This function should take a number and return it formatted as a currency string, e.g., `$100.00`.

module-8-account-tracker-project-sketch0.title: Project Overview
module-8-account-tracker-project-sketch0.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you'll build a multifile account tracker application. The project will involve creating a model file for account data, a helper file for formatting, and a main script to tie everything together. You'll learn how to define classes, use constructors, and apply encapsulation to manage object state effectively. This project will help you understand the importance of organizing code into separate files and using imports to maintain a clean and scalable codebase.
